chunks.text: Corporate Access Program - EECS at Berkeley Corporate Access Program (CAP) Our Corporate Access Program (CAP) provides exclusive access to EECS students through on-campus recruiting opportunities, events, and personalized staff support. A partnership with Berkeley EECS through CAP is the most seamless way to recruit on campus. Join CAP Build a connection to EECS. What is CAP? The Corporate Access Program was established to aid companies in recruiting and forming connections with students. CAP assists companies in: Managing competitive on-campus recruiting of EECS/CS students. Securing departmental space and staff time, alongside offering soft funding to student organizations and other departmental priorities. CAP assists students in fostering on-campus interactions with potential employers, ensuring these interactions are effectively organized, appropriately timed, and aligned with the university’s public mission. Host recruiting events on campus. Who is CAP for? CAP is most suitable for companies capable of frequent campus visits, adept at forming relationships with students in informal environments, and equipped with a strong system for gathering data and managing applicants. Companies that can highlight and leverage Berkeley alumni as recruiting partners are ideal matches. Recruit the brightest students in the industry. CAP Membership Benefits Develop your on-campus recruitment strategy tailored to your recruiting needs, including: A dedicated staff relationship manager to help you utilize the program benefits, customize your recruitment strategy, and serve as your entry point into UC Berkeley via EECS. At least two recruitment-focused events with a student organization’s consultation, partnership, and logistical assistance. Access to invite-only career fairs: The EECS

needs, including: A dedicated staff relationship manager to help you utilize the program benefits, customize your recruitment strategy, and serve as your entry point into UC Berkeley via EECS. At least two recruitment-focused events with a student organization’s consultation, partnership, and logistical assistance. Access to invite-only career fairs: The EECS Career Fair and EECS Internship Fair , in early September. On-campus interviews, office hours, and access to department resources. CAP At-A-Glance ~3,000 students attended CAP events in 2023-2024 22 CAP events in 2023-2024 On-Campus Recruiting Info sessions, tech talks, tabling, and interviews. CAP Annual Membership A $20,000 gift to the EECS Industrial Liaison Fund, annually, which funds student organizations, and special initiatives. 2025-2026 Student Fairs EECS Career Fair (Careers/Full-Time roles ONLY) – September 4, 2024 EECS Internship Fair (Internship roles ONLY) – September 5, 2024 “We love recruiting at Berkeley because of the awesome students and the partnership with staff. I have received so much positive feedback from the engineers we brought on campus... and that wouldn’t have been possible without CAP.” – Current CAP member Rising Stars in EECS Workshop moderator Sarah Bird and panelists Philip Wong (Stanford), Deborah Crawford (International Computer Science Institute), Kelin Kuhn (Cornell & Intel), Bill Dally (NVIDIA), Katherine Yellick (EECS), Eric Brewer (EECS) Photos Copyright Noah Berger / 2014 EECS + Industry View our list of CAP members “The Berkeley EECS Corporate Access Program is such an asset to our recruitment activities at Intel. The CAP team provides multiple opportunities to engage with students,

Bill Dally (NVIDIA), Katherine Yellick (EECS), Eric Brewer (EECS) Photos Copyright Noah Berger / 2014 EECS + Industry View our list of CAP members “The Berkeley EECS Corporate Access Program is such an asset to our recruitment activities at Intel. The CAP team provides multiple opportunities to engage with students, and goes out of their way to accommodate each company’s needs. We’re fortunate to partner with such a solid organization.” – Intel For more information, please contact: James Greene External Relations Coordinator External Relations Group (XRG) 231 Cory Hall #1770 University of California Berkeley, CA 94720 Email: cap-admin@eecs.berkeley.edu View Open Faculty Positions
